h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is being guided to name force arrows as interactions between an external object and the cart.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: Both lines — one object, one cart. Track card still under the tail.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner performs the new interaction-label criterion by checking that each force label names one acting object and the cart, anchored to the contact point.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence:
Only the thing touching here pushes.

Bench or track? Name the up arrow. Then both arrows again.

Page turned; interaction stayed.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: L1's bench-track uncertainty creates pressure, and T2 narrows attention to contact, but this remains a focused continuation of the same labeling route rather than a full adaptive mechanism with explicit stock-taking.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence:
Only the thing touching here pushes.

Bench or track? Name the up arrow. Then both arrows again.

Page turned; interaction stayed.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The contact cue is specific and fitted to the learner's bench-track confusion, but it is a narrowed prompt rather than a visibly new public device or transferable representation.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no recognitive learner pivot and no later tutor turn adapting to such a revised framing.
